archimate:BusinessObject.name: Data Quality Framework
archimate:BusinessObject.documentation:
Definition: A Data Quality Framework refers to a structured approach or set of guidelines that organizations use to assess, measure, and improve the quality of their data. It provides a systematic framework for managing data quality throughout its lifecycle, from data collection to data analysis and decision-making.
Source: European Commission
LOST view: SV-Governance Architecture Principles
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/DataQualityFrameworkBusinessObject
ABB name: egovera:DataQualityFrameworkBusinessObject
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/DataQualityFrameworkBusinessObject
Interoperability Layer: Semantic
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DataPolicy

properties.value: A Data Quality Framework refers to a structured approach or set of guidelines that organizations use to assess, measure, and improve the quality of their data. It provides a systematic framework for managing data quality throughout its lifecycle, from data collection to data analysis and decision-making.
